title: Rearrange Subitems in Automation with Drag & Drop
excerpt: I would like the ability to rearrange subitems within an automation using drag and drop. Currently, if you want to add a subitem to the top or middle of an automation, you have to delete all the subitems below it and the…

posts.author: MichelleHPREXP (Michelle Rud)
posts.cookedHtml:
I would like the ability to rearrange subitems within an automation using drag and drop. Currently, if you want to add a subitem to the top or middle of an automation, you have to delete all the subitems below it and then rebuild the automation.

While you can drag and drop items outside of the automation, this functionality is not available within the automation itself. This makes it difficult to edit the order of subitems, especially if you frequently update them. Being able to rearrange subitems easily within an automation would greatly improve efficiency.

posts.author: jaskable (J A)
posts.cookedHtml:
I totally agree with this!
The ability to add a step in the middle of an automation would be so helpful.
And the ability to rearrange them too so we don’t have to rebuild a whole automation when we just want to add a line.

posts.author: Fan8 (Fannie Huet)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello
Is there any news concerning this request?

Is it possible also to look into being able to move subitem places when adjust the automation? Or to be able to add a subitem in the middle of the automation?

I know we can add one in the board, but I am really talking about the automation, so it is set to all my new items I add.

Right now, for example, I have subitem creations, via automations, for around 20 subitems. Whenever I want to add a new subitem in the middle, for example, I would need to redo all the subsequent subitem. As we can add one in the middle or we cant move one up or down. So right now, for lack of time, I add them at the bottom. But then the order of all my tasks are all mess up.
